behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is most likely at the handoff between classifier logic and API client payload construction/serialization. A pure unit test of a helper can miss dropped or malformed fields at module wiring boundaries, while real external infra is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier to a spy/fake API client adapter, call classify(document), and assert the outbound request payload contains the full expected taxonomy set (no missing/extra labels) in the field consumed by the API client.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily local validation/mapping logic: given taxonomy + returned label, map out-of-taxonomy values to "unknown". No real module interaction or infra is required to expose the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Test the label-validation/mapping function directly with taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other] and returned label "medical"; assert output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: If correctness depends on real model output stability, this is an LLM non-determinism boundary. Mocked or deterministic stubs can falsely pass and hide real variance from the external AI model.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real API classifications (e.g., 30-100 calls) for the same document under production model/settings, then assert all returned labels are identical; fail on any label drift.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Real token accounting is provider/model-specific and can differ from local estimators. Mocking usage numbers can hide real over-budget failures; this needs a real API call and observed usage metadata.
behaviors.test_description: Send a representative document to the real API with production prompt/template, read returned usage/token metrics, and assert total consumed tokens are <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on HTTP-level rate-limit signaling plus timing/retry behavior. In-process mocks can miss real status/header parsing and wait semantics in the HTTP stack.
behaviors.test_description: Use a controllable HTTP test server that emulates the external API: first response 429 (with Retry-After), second response valid classification. Assert classifier waits at least the required delay, retries, and returns a successful label instead of surfacing an error.
